findings.recommendation: Delete status_colors and the _color assignment until the row-styling feature is actually implemented; re-add them together with the styling code when that work starts.

findings.title: Some exception handlers silently swallow failures while sibling methods surface them to the user
findings.detail: Confirmed: check_timezone_support (899-913) catches bare `except Exception:` and returns False with no logging (line 912-913), so a real connectivity/permission error during the `mysql.time_zone_name` query is indistinguishable from 'server genuinely lacks the table'. _get_dump_schema_name (923-936) likewise swallows any exception and returns "" silently (935-936). This is inconsistent with _run_upgrade_check (795-829) in the same class, which catches broadly but surfaces the failure via lbl_upgrade_status (lines 826-829), and with db_export_dialog.py's on_raw_output (lines 1221-1224), which also silently discards JSON-parse exceptions via `except Exception: event = None`.
findings.recommendation: At minimum log the swallowed exception (e.g. `logger.debug(...)`) before returning the fallback value in check_timezone_support and _get_dump_schema_name, so silent failures are diagnosable; consider narrowing the `except Exception` to the specific expected exception types where feasible.

findings.title: test_connection and do_connect duplicate field-reading, validation, connector-creation and error handling
findings.detail: Confirmed: test_connection (212-240) and do_connect (242-271) both read host/port/user/password/database from the same widgets (214-218 vs 244-248), validate `if not user` (220-222 vs 250-252), call QApplication.setOverrideCursor, build the connector via self._current_engine + self._create_connector, and wrap the connect call in an identical try/except that restores the cursor and shows QMessageBox.critical on exception - only the success-path behavior (info box + disconnect vs. store self.connector + accept()) differs.
findings.recommendation: Extract `_read_connection_fields() -> tuple` and `_build_connector_or_raise() -> connector`, and have test_connection/do_connect call these shared helpers, keeping only the success-path behavior distinct.

findings.title: _on_tunnel_selected and on_mode_changed duplicate the 'apply tunnel data to form fields' logic, with inconsistent defensiveness
findings.detail: Confirmed and confirmed the drift is real: _on_tunnel_selected (160-178) guards `if 'host' in current_data and 'port' in current_data:` (line 168) before writing input_host/input_port, while the tunnel branch of on_mode_changed (191-210) writes `tunnel_data['host']`/`tunnel_data['port']` unconditionally at lines 201-202 (no key-existence check) - so on_mode_changed would raise a KeyError where _on_tunnel_selected would silently skip. Both then identically check `'tunnel_id' in ...` before calling _fill_saved_credentials/_apply_engine_from_config and filling input_database from default_database/default_schema.
findings.recommendation: Extract a single `_apply_tunnel_data(self, tunnel_data: dict) -> None` method containing the guarded field-population logic (including the 'host'/'port' presence check), and call it from both _on_tunnel_selected and on_mode_changed.

findings.title: MigrationAnalyzerDialog mixes UI, SQL generation, file I/O, and worker orchestration in one ~1250-line class
findings.detail: Re-read confirms all cited responsibilities are present in this single class: 5 tab builders (init_ui 224-401, init_overview_tab 402-426, init_orphans_tab 427-529, init_compatibility_tab 531-597, init_fk_tree_tab 598-617, init_log_tab 619-639), worker lifecycle management in closeEvent (171-222) using module-level _detach_workers_until_finished, raw SQL text building in _generate_orphan_select_query (981-990) and SQL dispatch in on_orphan_selected (950-975), JSON/.sql/.txt persistence (_auto_save_result 1267-1283, save_analysis_result 1285-1317, _save_result_directly 1319-1343, load_analysis_result 1345-1390, export_orphan_queries 1019-1068, save_log 656-667), and the AUTO_FIXABLE_TYPES domain-classification constant (1159-1167). Counted ~37 methods on the class (excluding the property), consistent with the '~35 methods' claim.
findings.recommendation: Split into: (1) a thin MigrationAnalyzerDialog that only builds widgets and wires signals, (2) a MigrationResultStore/AnalysisResultRepository for save/load/auto-save JSON logic (_auto_save_result, save_analysis_result, _save_result_directly, load_analysis_result, export_orphan_queries), (3) move _generate_orphan_select_query and AUTO_FIXABLE_TYPES into src/core/migration_analyzer.py next to generate_cleanup_sql so the domain module is the single source of truth for 'what SQL do we generate' and 'what is auto-fixable'.

findings.title: AUTO_FIXABLE_TYPES set is verbatim duplicated in a second file
findings.detail: Confirmed: migration_dialogs.py:1159-1167 defines the 7-member AUTO_FIXABLE_TYPES class attribute, and fix_wizard_issue_selection_page.py:93-101 (inside populate_table) defines an identically-valued local `auto_fixable_types` set with the same 7 IssueType members in the same order. No import/shared constant links the two; nothing keeps them in sync.
findings.recommendation: Move the set to a single shared location (e.g. `MigrationAnalyzer.AUTO_FIXABLE_ISSUE_TYPES` or a constant in src/core/migration_constants.py) and have both migration_dialogs.py and fix_wizard_issue_selection_page.py import it instead of redefining it.

findings.title: IssueType -> Korean display-name dict is independently re-implemented 5 times across 4 files, with drifted text
findings.detail: Confirmed all 5 dict definitions and the specific drift: migration_dialogs.py:871-889 (filter_compatibility_issues) has RESERVED_KEYWORD='žėąžēĹžĖī', CHARSET_ISSUE='Ž¨łžěźžÖč', ZEROFILL_USAGE='ZEROFILL žÜćžĄĪ'. migration_manual_guide_dialog.py:199-205 (populate_issues) has RESERVED_KEYWORD='žėąžēĹžĖī ž∂©ŽŹĆ' (confirmed drift). fix_wizard_issue_selection_page.py:103-114 (populate_table) has CHARSET_ISSUE='Ž¨łžěźžÖč', ZEROFILL_USAGE='ZEROFILL' (confirmed drift vs 'Ž¨łžěźžÖč žĚīžäą'/'ZEROFILL žÜćžĄĪ' elsewhere). fix_wizard_option_page.py:59-68 (BatchOptionDialog.init_ui) has CHARSET_ISSUE='Ž¨łžěźžÖč žĚīžäą', ZEROFILL_USAGE='ZEROFILL žÜćžĄĪ'. fix_wizard_option_page.py:362-370 (FixOptionPage.show_current_issue) has a third variant with extra qualifiers: INVALID_DATE='žěėŽ™ĽŽźú Žā†žßú (0000-00-00)', FLOAT_PRECISION='FLOAT ž†ēŽįÄŽŹĄ ÍĶ¨Ž¨ł', DEPRECATED_ENGINE='deprecated žä§ŪÜ†Ž¶¨žßÄ žóĒžßĄ', ENUM_EMPTY_VALUE='ENUM ŽĻą Ž¨łžěźžóī'. All cited drift examples check out exactly.
findings.recommendation: Create one canonical `ISSUE_TYPE_DISPLAY_NAMES: Dict[IssueType, str]` constant (e.g. in src/core/migration_constants.py) covering every IssueType, and have all 4 files import and use it instead of maintaining separate ad-hoc dicts. Reconcile the drifted labels as part of the consolidation.

findings.title: Inline QPushButton stylesheet blocks are copy-pasted 7 times in this file (and again per-file in the rest of the fix-wizard/oneclick dialogs)
findings.detail: Confirmed 7 inline QSS blocks at the cited line ranges (btn_analyze 303-311, btn_oneclick 317-325, btn_close 388-394, btn_dry_run 483-490, btn_execute 496-503, btn_auto_fix 558-565, btn_manual_guide 573-580), and confirmed matching copy-pasted patterns in oneclick_migration_dialog.py (btn_start ~726-734), fix_wizard_option_page.py (btn_batch_apply 254-260), fix_wizard_preview_page.py (btn_dryrun 73-80), and fix_wizard_execution_page.py (btn_execute 127-135). One caveat: btn_close (388-394) is structurally a bit different from the others ‚ÄĒ it has no `font-weight: bold` and no `:disabled` state (it's a secondary/neutral button, not an action button), so 'identical structure ... differing only in 2-3 color values' slightly overstates that one case. The core duplication finding and recommendation are still valid for the other 6+ call sites.
findings.recommendation: Extract a helper, e.g. `make_action_button(text, bg, hover, disabled='#bdc3c7') -> QPushButton` in a shared ui/styles.py or ui/widgets.py, and use it at every one of these call sites (treat btn_close as a separate `make_secondary_button` variant since it lacks bold/disabled styling). This turns ~10 seven/eight-line QSS blocks into one-line calls and removes an entire class of copy/paste color-typo risk.
findings.verification_note: Minor overstatement: btn_close's QSS block lacks font-weight:bold and :disabled, unlike the other 6 cited buttons. Does not undermine the finding, but the helper extraction should treat it as a distinct button variant.

findings.title: Several imports are unused (re, html, QFont, QLineEdit, QSpinBox, QListWidget, QListWidgetItem, QMenu, QSplitter)
findings.detail: Confirmed via search: none of `re.`, `html.`, `QFont(`, `QLineEdit(`, `QSpinBox(`, `QListWidget(`, `QListWidgetItem(`, `QMenu(`, `QSplitter(` appear anywhere else in the file, yet `import re` (line 11), `import html` (line 10), `QFont` (line 24), and `QLineEdit`, `QSpinBox`, `QListWidget`, `QListWidgetItem`, `QMenu`, `QSplitter` (lines 15-21) are all imported. save_log() at line 658 does re-import `QFileDialog` locally even though it's already imported at module scope (line 21) ‚ÄĒ confirmed.
findings.recommendation: Remove the unused imports `import html` (line 10), `import re` (line 11), `QFont` (line 24), and the unused Qt widget imports `QLineEdit`, `QSpinBox`, `QListWidget`, `QListWidgetItem`, `QMenu`, `QSplitter` (lines 15-21). Also drop the redundant local `from PyQt6.QtWidgets import QFileDialog` inside save_log() (line 658) since QFileDialog is already imported at module scope on line 21.

findings.title: QScrollArea and QColor are imported but never used
findings.detail: Confirmed: `QScrollArea` (line 15, QtWidgets import) and `QColor` (line 18, QtGui import) are imported, and a search for `QScrollArea(` / `QColor(` across the file (994 lines total, matching the '995-line file' claim) returns no matches ‚ÄĒ all colors are applied via inline stylesheet strings.
findings.recommendation: Remove the unused `QScrollArea` import from the QtWidgets import block and the unused `QColor` import from the QtGui import block.

findings.title: FixWizardWorker, CharsetTableInfo and BatchOptionDialog are imported but never referenced
findings.detail: Confirmed on this 157-line file: `CharsetTableInfo` (line 20), `FixWizardWorker` (line 21), and `BatchOptionDialog` (line 24) each appear only in their own import statement and nowhere else in the file ‚ÄĒ grep for all three names found zero other occurrences.
findings.recommendation: Drop the unused imports: remove `CharsetTableInfo` from the migration_fix_wizard import, remove the `from src.ui.workers.fix_wizard_worker import FixWizardWorker` line entirely, and import only `FixOptionPage` from fix_wizard_option_page (drop `BatchOptionDialog`).

findings.title: FK-tree traversal (root-finding, cycle detection, rendered-set bookkeeping) is implemented twice for two output formats
findings.detail: Confirmed: module-level `_format_fk_tree_text()` (lines 107-143) and the nested `add_tree_items()` inside `update_fk_tree()` (lines 907-948, walk logic at 924-936) both independently compute root_tables via set subtraction, track a `rendered`/`visited` set to guard against cycles, emit a cycle marker ('ūüĒĄ ... (žąúŪôė žįłž°į)'), and re-walk unreached tables as extra roots. The algorithm is duplicated line-for-line, differing only in the output sink (text lines vs QTreeWidgetItem).
findings.recommendation: Extract a single generator, e.g. `iter_fk_tree(fk_tree) -> Iterator[tuple[table, depth, is_cycle, is_last]]`, that performs the root-finding/cycle-detection/rendered-tracking once, and have both `_format_fk_tree_text` and `update_fk_tree` consume it to build their respective output (text lines vs QTreeWidgetItem).

findings.title: Orphan-count color thresholds (1000, 100) are unexplained magic numbers
findings.detail: Confirmed exact code: `if orphan.orphan_count > 1000: count_item.setForeground(QColor("#e74c3c")) elif orphan.orphan_count > 100: count_item.setForeground(QColor("#f39c12"))` at lines 823-826, with no named constant or comment explaining the thresholds.
findings.recommendation: Extract named constants, e.g. `ORPHAN_COUNT_CRITICAL_THRESHOLD = 1000` and `ORPHAN_COUNT_WARNING_THRESHOLD = 100`, at module scope near the color constants, so the thresholds are self-documenting and easy to tune in one place.

findings.title: init_ui() is a ~180-line function building two full option rows, three buttons with inline stylesheets, and the tab widget
findings.detail: Confirmed init_ui spans exactly lines 224-401 (177 lines) and directly builds the schema selector, two full checkbox rows (5 + 4 checkboxes with individual setChecked/setToolTip calls), the 'analyze'/'one-click'/'save'/'load' buttons (2 with inline QSS), the tab widget assembly (5 tabs), and the bottom close button (with inline QSS) ‚ÄĒ all at the same nesting level, unlike the tab bodies which are already split into init_overview_tab/init_orphans_tab/etc.
findings.recommendation: Split init_ui into smaller helpers mirroring the existing tab-init pattern, e.g. `_build_schema_row()`, `_build_basic_check_options()`, `_build_upgrade_checker_options()`, `_build_action_buttons()`, each returning a layout/widget to add to the top-level layout.

findings.title: MigrationAnalyzerWorker is constructed with 16 individual keyword arguments, 14 of them booleans
findings.detail: Confirmed the call site at lines 714-733 passes exactly 16 keyword arguments: `connector`, `schema`, plus 14 `check_*` booleans (check_orphans, check_charset, check_keywords, check_routines, check_sql_mode, check_auth_plugins, check_zerofill, check_float_precision, check_fk_name_length, check_invalid_dates, check_year2, check_deprecated_engines, check_enum_empty, check_timestamp_range). The original finding said '15 of them booleans' ‚ÄĒ recounted and corrected to 14 (connector and schema are the 2 non-boolean args). Confirmed 5 of the booleans (check_invalid_dates, check_year2, check_deprecated_engines, check_enum_empty, check_timestamp_range) are hardcoded to True with no corresponding checkbox anywhere in the file (grep for chk_invalid_dates/chk_year2/chk_deprecated_engines/chk_enum_empty/chk_timestamp_range found nothing).
findings.recommendation: Introduce an `AnalysisOptions` dataclass (or a dict built once from the checkbox states) with named fields, and pass a single `options=AnalysisOptions(...)` argument to the worker. This also makes it obvious which checks are user-configurable vs always-on.
findings.verification_note: Corrected boolean count from 15 to 14 (16 total args minus connector and schema = 14 booleans). The rest of the finding, including the 'always-True and unwired' claim, is accurate.

findings.title: Dialog directly builds raw SQL text for orphan-record SELECT queries instead of delegating to the analyzer/core module
findings.detail: Confirmed `_generate_orphan_select_query()` (lines 981-990) hand-assembles a LEFT JOIN + IS NULL f-string SQL block directly in the dialog, while `on_orphan_selected` (line 972) and `execute_cleanup` (line 1098) already delegate cleanup-SQL generation to `MigrationAnalyzer.generate_cleanup_sql()` a few lines above. The SELECT-preview path is the one case that rolls its own SQL text instead of asking the analyzer.
findings.recommendation: Add a `MigrationAnalyzer.generate_orphan_select_sql(orphan, schema)` (or equivalent Rust-core call) alongside the existing `generate_cleanup_sql`, and have the dialog call that instead of building SQL text itself. Keeps all SQL-shape knowledge in one module.

findings.title: generate_sql_preview() performs SQL templating/deduplication business logic inside a QWizardPage
findings.detail: Confirmed generate_sql_preview (lines 92-171) calls FKSafeCharsetChanger for charset SQL, then for the 'other issues' section performs `{custom_date}`/`{precision}` placeholder substitution on `step.selected_option.sql_template` (lines 150-153) and de-duplicates identical rendered SQL via `hash(sql)` in a `processed_sql_hashes` set (lines 146, 156-159) ‚ÄĒ both are migration-domain concerns embedded in a UI preview method, with no equivalent helper in src/core/migration_fix_wizard.py.
findings.recommendation: Move the placeholder substitution (`sql.replace('{custom_date}', ...)`) and the dedup-by-hash logic into a `FixWizardStep`/`migration_fix_wizard` helper, e.g. `step.rendered_sql()` and `render_all_steps_sql(steps)`, so the wizard page only formats/concatenates already-rendered strings for display.

findings.title: Duck-typed 'CombinedExecutionResult vs BatchExecutionResult' branch is duplicated between ExecutionPage and PreviewPage
findings.detail: Confirmed `if hasattr(result, 'charset_tables_count'): ... else: ...` at lines 207/208-226 in on_finished() re-derives total items/success/fail/affected-rows fields from whichever result type is present, and the structurally identical pattern exists in fix_wizard_preview_page.py's on_dryrun_finished (lines 208-220, `if hasattr(result, 'charset_tables_count')`). Both independently sniff the same attribute rather than using a shared accessor.
findings.recommendation: Give both result classes a common method/property, e.g. `summary() -> ExecutionSummary(total, success, fail, affected_rows)`, so both wizard pages call `result.summary()` instead of each re-implementing the `hasattr` branch and field extraction.
findings.verification_note: Tightened line_end from 227 (a blank line) to 226 (last line of the duck-typed branch, `fail_count = result.fail_count`).

findings.title: isComplete() always returns True ‚ÄĒ the conditional branch is pointless
findings.detail: The finding's file attribution was wrong: this exact code (`if not self.wizard_dialog.wizard_steps: return True` / `return True  # žėĶžÖė žĄ†ŪÉĚžĚÄ validatePagežóźžĄú Í≤Äž¶Ě`) does NOT exist in fix_wizard_issue_selection_page.py ‚ÄĒ that file's isComplete() (lines 211-220) does real, meaningful logic based on checked/hidden table rows. The literal code described is actually FixOptionPage.isComplete() in src/ui/dialogs/fix_wizard_option_page.py, lines 498-503, confirmed verbatim: both branches return the same literal `True`, so the `if not self.wizard_dialog.wizard_steps` check has no effect on behavior.
findings.recommendation: Simplify to `def isComplete(self) -> bool: return True  # validation happens in validatePage()` and drop the dead branch. Applies to src/ui/dialogs/fix_wizard_option_page.py (FixOptionPage class), not fix_wizard_issue_selection_page.py.
findings.verification_note: File corrected from src/ui/dialogs/fix_wizard_issue_selection_page.py to src/ui/dialogs/fix_wizard_option_page.py ‚ÄĒ the described code exists verbatim there (lines 498-503) but not in the originally cited file, whose isComplete() is unrelated, real logic (lines 211-220). The underlying finding is accurate once the file is corrected.

findings.title: nextId() branches on has_charset_issues() but both branches resolve identically based only on has_other_issues()
findings.detail: Confirmed by tracing all 4 combinations of has_charset_issues()/has_other_issues() against the code at lines 378-395: in every case the return value is preview_page_id when has_other_issues() is False and option_page_id when it is True, regardless of has_charset_issues(). The outer branch is provably dead weight.
findings.recommendation: Collapse to: `return self.wizard_dialog.option_page_id if self.wizard_dialog.has_other_issues() else self.wizard_dialog.preview_page_id`, removing the redundant `has_charset_issues()` branch entirely (or add a comment explaining why it's intentionally kept if some future differentiation is planned).

findings.title: 'žõźŽ≥ł žĚīžäą' / 'FK žóįÍīÄ' tag labels duplicate an identical stylesheet block save for one color
findings.detail: Confirmed: the `if info.is_original_issue: ... else: ...` block at lines 187-210 builds two QLabel tag widgets with byte-for-byte identical QSS (`padding: 2px 6px; border-radius: 3px; font-size: 10px; color: white;`), differing only in `background-color` (#e74c3c vs #3498db) and the label text ('žõźŽ≥ł žĚīžäą' vs 'FK žóįÍīÄ').
findings.recommendation: Extract a small `_make_tag_label(text, color) -> QLabel` helper and call it with the two (text, color) pairs instead of duplicating the QSS block.

findings.title: Local variable rollback_dir shadows the imported rollback_dir() function
findings.detail: Confirmed: `from src.core.platform_paths import rollback_dir` is imported at module scope (line 14), and `_save_and_show_rollback()` assigns `rollback_dir = self._get_rollback_dir()` (line 260) inside the same method, shadowing the imported function for the rest of that scope. It works today because the function isn't called again after the shadow, but any future edit that tries to call `rollback_dir()` again in this method would silently call the string variable instead and raise `TypeError: 'str' object is not callable`.
findings.recommendation: Rename the local variable, e.g. `rollback_dir_path = self._get_rollback_dir()`, to avoid colliding with the imported `rollback_dir` function name.

findings.title: _rollback_sql_content is never declared in __init__ and is only discoverable via scattered hasattr checks
findings.detail: Confirmed: unlike `self.executed` and `self.rollback_sql_path`, which are initialized in `__init__` (lines 25-26), `self._rollback_sql_content` is first assigned ad hoc inside `_save_and_show_rollback` (lines 268 and 286) and guarded with `hasattr(self, '_rollback_sql_content')` in `copy_rollback_sql` (line 307) and `save_rollback_as` (line 316).
findings.recommendation: Declare `self._rollback_sql_content: Optional[str] = None` alongside `self.rollback_sql_path` in `__init__`, then simplify the three call sites to a plain `if self._rollback_sql_content:` check instead of `hasattr(...)`.

findings.title: FixOptionPage docstring advertises FK-tree visualization and auto-include features that the code explicitly says were removed
findings.detail: Confirmed: the class docstring (lines 191-200) lists 'FK žóįÍīÄ ŪÖĆžĚīŽłĒ Tree žčúÍįĀŪôĒ', 'FK žóįÍīÄŪÖĆžĚīŽłĒ žĚľÍīĄ Ž≥ÄÍ≤Ĺ žčú žěźŽŹô ŪŹ¨Ūē® (žėĶžÖė žĄ†ŪÉĚŽßĆ ÍĪīŽĄąŽúÄ)', and 'žěźŽŹô ŪŹ¨Ūē®Žźú ŪÖĆžĚīŽłĒ ÍĪīŽĄąŽõįÍłį ŽĄ§ŽĻĄÍ≤ĆžĚīžÖė' as current features. But init_ui()'s own comment at lines 270-273 states these were 'dead code' from when the page used to also handle charset issues and were removed. Confirmed no Tree widget or auto-include navigation code exists anywhere else in this file.
findings.recommendation: Update the class docstring to drop the 3 stale bullet points (FK Tree visualization, FK auto-include, auto-included-table skip navigation) and describe only what the page currently does (per-issue option selection + batch-apply dialog), to avoid a future reader believing those features still exist.

findings.title: CrossEngineMigrationDialog mixes widget construction, wizard state machine, and Rust-payload interpretation in one 1450-line class
findings.detail: Confirmed by direct read of the full file (1509 lines total). The class spans exactly lines 43-1499 (closeEvent's `a0.accept()` on 1499 is the last statement before the module-level `CrossEngineMigrationWizard` class at 1504). Four responsibility clusters are all present and correctly located: (1) UI construction in `_setup_ui` (180-475, confirmed) plus the 83-line inline stylesheet in `_apply_wizard_style` (96-178, confirmed) -- one correction: the class builds 7 QGroupBoxes (option_group, schema_group, execution_group, safety_group, action_group, plan_group, verify_group), not '~10' as originally stated; the ~20-28 `.connect(...)` signal wiring count in `_setup_ui` is accurate. (2) Wizard step machine: `step_ids`(66-73), `_step_completed`(85-91), `_next_enabled_for_current_step`(770-785), `_show_step`(840-851), `_go_next_step`(858-868) all confirmed present. (3) Rust-payload text formatting duplicating `render_result_report`: `_schema_summary_text`(491-516), `_plan_summary_text`(544-575), `_verification_result_text`(593-644), `_update_migration_result_summary`(1334-1389) all confirmed at the exact stated line ranges, each hand-building strings from raw payload dicts. (4) Worker lifecycle: `_start_command`(923-956), `_start_command_with_payload`(958-982), `_on_result`(990-1046), `_on_finished`(1081-1118) all confirmed.
findings.recommendation: Split into (a) a WizardStepController owning step_ids/_step_completed/navigation (_show_step, _go_next_step, _go_previous_step, _next_enabled_for_current_step, _refresh_navigation_state), (b) a result-presentation module co-located with render_result_report in src/core/cross_engine_migration.py that owns _schema_summary_text/_plan_summary_text/_verification_result_text/_update_migration_result_summary's formatting logic, and (c) keep CrossEngineMigrationDialog as a thin view wiring the two together plus widget creation and worker signal plumbing.
findings.verification_note: Confirmed accurate; only correction is the QGroupBox count (7 actual vs '~10' claimed). Severity 'high' is justified given the confirmed 1450+ line span with four genuinely separable, actively-coupled responsibility clusters.

findings.title: Next/Previous button enable-state logic is re-derived identically in three separate methods
findings.detail: Confirmed. `_refresh_navigation_state` (805-813, the method body ends at 813; 814 is a blank line) computes `btn_previous.setEnabled(self._current_step_index() > 0 and not running)` and `btn_next` state from `_next_enabled_for_current_step()`. `_show_step` (840-851) duplicates the identical block verbatim at lines 844-849, then calls `self._refresh_navigation_state()` at line 850 -- confirmed this immediately re-runs the exact same computation right after the inline copy. `_set_running` (1198-1220) has a third copy at lines 1217-1220, and the stylistic drift is confirmed exactly as described: `_set_running` writes `(not running) and self._current_step_index() > 0` (line 1218) while `_refresh_navigation_state` writes `self._current_step_index() > 0 and not running` (line 808) -- same predicate, operands swapped, proving the copies have already diverged.
findings.recommendation: Delete the redundant inline block in `_show_step` (lines 844-849) since the trailing `self._refresh_navigation_state()` call on line 850 already does the same work. Replace lines 1217-1220 in `_set_running` with a single trailing call to `self._refresh_navigation_state()` instead of a third copy of the condition.
findings.verification_note: Confirmed accurate, including the exact operand-order drift between the two conditionals. Minor correction: _refresh_navigation_state's real body is 805-813 (814 is blank, not part of the method).

findings.title: Three different hard-coded thread-wait timeouts (5000/3000/1000 ms) with no shared constant or documented rationale
findings.detail: Confirmed exactly. `_wait_for_worker_finish` (1120-1128) calls `worker.wait(5000)` at line 1124. `closeEvent` (1482-1499) calls `self.worker.wait(3000)` at line 1496, and if that returns False, calls `self.worker.terminate()` (1497) followed by `self.worker.wait(1000)` at line 1498. No comment anywhere explains why a normal finish gets 5s of patience while a user-triggered close only gets 3s before a 1s force-terminate wait.
findings.recommendation: Extract named module-level constants, e.g. `WORKER_GRACEFUL_WAIT_MS = 5000`, `WORKER_CLOSE_WAIT_MS = 3000`, `WORKER_TERMINATE_WAIT_MS = 1000`, with a one-line comment explaining the intended difference (or unify them to one constant if the difference isn't actually intentional).
findings.verification_note: Confirmed accurate at the exact stated line numbers (1124, 1496, 1498).

findings.title: btn_run_plan/btn_run_verify are aliases of btn_plan/btn_verify, giving the same widgets two names
findings.detail: Confirmed exactly. Line 363: `self.btn_run_plan = self.btn_plan`. Line 437: `self.btn_run_verify = self.btn_verify`. Both alias names are then used interchangeably: `plan_layout.addWidget(self.btn_run_plan)` (401) and `verify_action_layout.addWidget(self.btn_run_verify)` (439), while the `.clicked.connect(...)` wiring at lines 382 and 385 uses `self.btn_plan`/`self.btn_verify` directly.
findings.recommendation: Keep the canonical names `btn_plan`/`btn_verify` (since those already carry the `.clicked` connections at lines 382/385), remove the alias assignments at lines 363 and 437, and update the two `addWidget` call sites (401, 439) to use `self.btn_plan`/`self.btn_verify` directly.
findings.verification_note: Confirmed accurate at the exact stated line numbers.

findings.title: _on_result is a 57-line dispatcher handling 7 unrelated command types inline
findings.detail: Confirmed exactly. `_on_result` spans lines 990-1046 (the next method, `_append_readiness_summary`, starts at 1048). It branches on `payload.get("command")` for: schema/inspect handling (999-1019), `readiness` (1020-1021), `guide` (1022-1023), `plan` (1024-1026), `verify` (1027-1030), the combined `preflight`/`plan` safety-unlock block (1031-1040), and `migrate` (1041-1043) -- all confirmed present and each mutating shared dialog state plus UI widgets directly from the same payload dict.
findings.recommendation: Introduce a per-command handler map, e.g. `handlers = {'plan': self._handle_plan_result, 'verify': self._handle_verify_result, 'preflight': self._handle_preflight_result, 'migrate': self._handle_migrate_result, 'readiness': self._handle_readiness_result, 'guide': self._handle_guide_result}`, and dispatch via `handlers.get(payload.get('command'), self._handle_generic_result)(payload)`, moving each branch body (990-1046) into its own small method.
findings.verification_note: Confirmed accurate at the exact stated line numbers; severity 'medium' is appropriate given branching complexity across 7 command types rather than raw line count alone.

findings.title: Postgres/MySQL defaults ("public", "postgres", 3306, 5432) are hard-coded in five+ separate spots with no shared constant
findings.detail: Confirmed. "public" appears at line 174 (`_on_engine_changed`), line 210 (`_apply_tunnel_data`), and line 287 (`payload`), plus again in `cross_engine_migration_dialog.py:1253` (`_target_approval_schema`: `return schema or "public"`) -- confirmed all four. "postgres" as a default database appears at line 201 (confirmed) and again at line 289 in `payload()` (`database = "postgres"`, not previously listed but a real fifth occurrence). Ports 3306/5432 are duplicated between `__init__` line 43 (`self.input_port.setValue(3306 if default_engine == DatabaseEngine.MYSQL else 5432)`) and `_on_engine_changed` lines 167-170 (confirmed both).
findings.recommendation: Add shared constants in src/core/cross_engine_migration.py: `DEFAULT_MYSQL_PORT = 3306`, `DEFAULT_POSTGRESQL_PORT = 5432`, `DEFAULT_POSTGRESQL_SCHEMA = "public"`, `DEFAULT_POSTGRESQL_DATABASE = "postgres"`, and import them from both cross_engine_migration_endpoint_form.py (lines 43, 174, 201, 210, 287, 289) and cross_engine_migration_dialog.py:1253.
findings.verification_note: Confirmed accurate; found one additional undocumented occurrence of the "postgres" default at line 289 that strengthens rather than weakens the finding.

findings.title: _apply_tunnel_data runs two only-loosely-parallel POSTGRESQL/default_schema condition chains that are easy to get out of sync
findings.detail: Confirmed. The method spans lines 182-217 exactly. First chain (196-203): `if self.engine() == DatabaseEngine.POSTGRESQL and default_database:` (198) / `elif self.engine() == DatabaseEngine.POSTGRESQL:` (200, sets "postgres") / `elif default_schema:` (202) sets `input_database`. Second, separate chain (204-210): `if default_schema:` (204) with nested `if MYSQL` (205) / `elif POSTGRESQL` (207), then a separate `elif self.engine() == DatabaseEngine.POSTGRESQL and not self.input_schema.text().strip():` (209) sets `input_schema`. One correction to the original count: there are 4 total `self.engine() == DatabaseEngine.POSTGRESQL` checks across the two chains (lines 198, 200, 207, 209), not 3 as originally stated; the '2 separate default_schema truthiness checks' (lines 202, 204) is accurate.
findings.recommendation: Extract a pure helper `_resolve_default_database_and_schema(engine, default_database, default_schema) -> tuple[str, str]` that computes both fields from one set of conditions in one place, then assign `input_database`/`input_schema` in `_apply_tunnel_data` from its two return values.
findings.verification_note: Finding is substantively correct; corrected the POSTGRESQL-check count from 'three total' to four (lines 198, 200, 207, 209).

findings.title: _detect_engine(self, host, port, config) never uses its host/port parameters
findings.detail: Confirmed exactly. Lines 219-223: `def _detect_engine(self, host, port, config: Dict) -> DatabaseEngine:` reads only `config.get("db_engine")` and falls back to `self.engine()`; `host`/`port` are never referenced in the body. The call site at line 191 (`engine = self._detect_engine(host, port, config)`) passes both arguments in, confirming they are accepted but discarded.
findings.recommendation: Drop the unused host/port parameters from _detect_engine's signature (219) and update the call site at line 191 to `self._detect_engine(config)`, unless port-based detection (e.g. 3306‚Üímysql / 5432‚Üípostgresql) was actually intended, in which case implement it instead of silently ignoring the arguments.
findings.verification_note: Confirmed accurate at the exact stated line numbers.

findings.title: make_connection_payload(...) is called with 7 positional primitive arguments
findings.detail: Confirmed. The call at lines 290-298 passes `engine, host, port, user, password, database, schema` positionally, matching make_connection_payload's actual signature in src/core/cross_engine_migration.py:238-246 (`engine, host, port, user, password, database, schema=""`) -- currently correctly ordered, but the risk is latent rather than an active bug. Lines 283-289 immediately above do reassign `database`/`schema` from each other for MySQL (`database = schema or database; schema = database`) and default them for Postgres, exactly as described, meaning several same-typed string locals are actively shuffled right before the positional call.
findings.recommendation: Call with explicit keyword arguments at the existing call site (lines 290-298): `make_connection_payload(engine=self.engine(), host=self.input_host.text().strip(), port=self.input_port.value(), user=self.input_user.text().strip(), password=self.input_password.text(), database=database, schema=schema)`, or have make_connection_payload (src/core/cross_engine_migration.py:238) accept a small ConnectionPayloadInput dataclass built once in this method.
findings.verification_note: Confirmed accurate; verified make_connection_payload's actual 7-parameter signature in src/core/cross_engine_migration.py:238-246 matches the call site order exactly, so the current call is not miswired today -- the finding is about latent transposition risk, not a live bug, which the title/detail already convey correctly.

findings.title: SchemaDiffDialog owns UI layout, raw DB-connector lifecycle, thread orchestration, and result-tree rendering all in one class
findings.detail: Confirmed. The class spans lines 38-799 (file is 800 lines total, class is effectively the entire file). `_setup_ui` (72-215) builds the full comparison UI. The class directly constructs MySQLConnector instances at lines 356 and 364, and disconnects/clears them at 337-348 (pre-cleanup), 378-389 (exception handler), and 746-759 (closeEvent). It also owns two QThread subclasses via `_compare_thread`, `_schema_load_threads`, `_pending_schema_threads` plus `_cancel_compare_thread`/`_cancel_schema_load_threads`, and `_display_results` (starting at line 499) renders three parallel diff-kind result trees (column/index/FK).
findings.recommendation: Extract a small SchemaCompareSession helper (e.g. in a new src/ui/dialogs/diff_dialog_session.py) that owns _source_connector/_target_connector creation, teardown, and the 'clear stale connectors before a new compare' logic currently duplicated at lines 337-348, 378-389, and 746-759, so SchemaDiffDialog only calls session.start_compare(...) / session.close() and focuses on presentation.
findings.verification_note: Confirmed accurate; class span and all four cited responsibility clusters verified directly in the file.

findings.title: Source/target connector disconnect-and-clear block is copy-pasted 3 times
findings.detail: Confirmed. The identical `try: disconnect() / except Exception: pass / self._x_connector = None` pair for both connectors appears at: the pre-cleanup in `_start_compare` (337-348), the exception handler a few lines later in the same method (378-389), and again in `closeEvent` (746-759, one line later than the originally cited 746-758 since the block runs through line 759, not 758).
findings.recommendation: Extract a single `_disconnect_connectors(self)` method (clearing both `_source_connector` and `_target_connector` with try/except/None-out) and call it from all three sites: the `_start_compare` pre-cleanup (337-348), its exception handler (378-389), and `closeEvent` (746-759).
findings.verification_note: Confirmed accurate; minor correction that the third (closeEvent) occurrence runs through line 759, not 758 as originally stated.

findings.title: Column/index/FK diff-rendering loops in _display_results are near-identical copy-paste blocks
findings.detail: Confirmed exactly. The loop over `diff.column_diffs` (536-549), `diff.index_diffs` (552-571), and `diff.fk_diffs` (574-593) each independently compute `col_icon`/`sev_icon` via `_get_diff_icon`/`_get_severity_icon`, build a label string, construct a QTreeWidgetItem, call `setData`/`_apply_severity_background`/`addChild` -- differing only in the attribute name (column_name vs index_name vs fk_name) and that the index/FK loops additionally handle RENAMED old-name formatting (which the column loop omits).
findings.recommendation: Extract one helper, e.g. `_add_diff_child_item(self, parent_item, kind_prefix, diff, name_attr)` called from all three loops (536-549, 552-571, 574-593), so a new diff kind or a label/severity formatting change only needs to be made in one place.
findings.verification_note: Confirmed accurate at the exact stated line numbers.

findings.title: math, random, and QApplication are imported but never used in this file
findings.detail: Confirmed via direct grep of the file: `import math` (line 7) and `import random` (line 8) have zero `math.`/`random.` call sites anywhere else in diff_dialog.py; `QApplication` is imported in the PyQt6.QtWidgets list (line 16) but never referenced again. Cross-checked the claimed real usages: `random.uniform`/`random.random`/`random.choice` and `math.sin` are indeed used in src/ui/dialogs/diff_pixel_loading_widget.py, and `QApplication.clipboard()` is used in src/ui/dialogs/diff_sync_script_dialog.py, confirming these are leftover imports from before those pieces were split out.
findings.recommendation: Remove the three unused imports: `import math` (line 7), `import random` (line 8), and `QApplication` from the PyQt6.QtWidgets import list (line 16).
findings.verification_note: Confirmed accurate via grep of this file plus the two files claimed to hold the real usages.

findings.title: Diff-kind detection via hasattr() chains instead of isinstance/an explicit type tag
findings.detail: Confirmed exactly. `_show_diff_detail` spans lines 677-709 and distinguishes column/index/FK diff objects purely via `hasattr(diff, 'column_name')` (681), `hasattr(diff, 'index_name')` (683), `hasattr(diff, 'fk_name')` (685), plus further hasattr checks for `old_name` (691), `severity` (694), `source_info` (703), `target_info` (706) -- none of these branch on the object's actual class or an explicit discriminator field.
findings.recommendation: Add an explicit `kind` field (e.g. `Literal["column","index","fk"]`) to the ColumnDiff/IndexDiff/ForeignKeyDiff dataclasses in src/core/schema_diff.py, or branch on `isinstance(diff, ColumnDiff)` / `IndexDiff` / `ForeignKeyDiff` in `_show_diff_detail` (677-709) instead of hasattr.
findings.verification_note: Confirmed accurate at the exact stated line numbers.

findings.title: SettingsDialog owns 6 unrelated subsystems in a single ~1038-line, 31-method class
findings.detail: Confirmed by reading the full file: the class body runs exactly from line 42 (`class SettingsDialog(QDialog):`) to line 1079 (EOF) and contains 31 method definitions (including `__init__`/`init_ui`; 29 if you exclude those two orchestration methods). It genuinely mixes: general app settings (language/close-behavior/theme persistence, lines 91-357), GitHub App auto-report config + a live connection test (`_check_github_app`, `_test_github_connection`, lines 1015-1078), config backup/export/import file I/O (`_refresh_backup_list`/`_restore_selected_backup`/`_export_config`/`_import_config`, lines 359-441), a log viewer with level filtering and file clearing (`_create_log_tab`...`_clear_log_file`, lines 442-568), and a full self-update pipeline: check -> background download via QThread worker -> progress reporting -> installer launch via `subprocess.Popen` with active-tunnel warning (`_check_for_updates`...`_launch_installer`, lines 805-1006). None of these concerns depend on each other but all share one class's state.
findings.recommendation: Split into per-tab composer classes (e.g. GeneralSettingsTab, LogViewerTab, AboutUpdateTab), each owning its own widgets and emitting signals; reduce SettingsDialog to composing the three tabs plus Save/Cancel wiring. Move the update check/download/install orchestration into a dedicated UpdateManager service that SettingsDialog only observes.
findings.verification_note: Re-read src/ui/dialogs/settings.py in full. Line range and the 6-subsystem description are accurate. Corrected the method count from 'the finding didn't specify' to the actual 31 (29 excluding __init__/init_ui) since the title needed a concrete number to stay honest.

findings.title: _create_general_tab builds 7 independent setting groups inline in one 267-line method
findings.detail: Confirmed exact: the method runs lines 91-357 (267 lines) and constructs, in sequence and against one shared `layout` variable: the language group (96-114), close-behavior radio group (117-143), theme group (146-172), GitHub auto-report group with conditional test-button/description branching on `_github_app_configured` (175-225), backup/restore group with list widget + 3 buttons + refresh call (228-300), auto-reconnect group (303-333), and startup group (336-353) ‚ÄĒ that is 7 groups, not 6 as the original title stated (the body of the finding, and its own recommendation, already correctly enumerate 7: language/close-behavior/theme/github/backup/reconnect/startup).
findings.recommendation: Extract one private builder per QGroupBox (_build_language_group(), _build_close_behavior_group(), _build_theme_group(), _build_github_group(), _build_backup_group(), _build_reconnect_group(), _build_startup_group()), each returning a widget; have _create_general_tab just assemble the returned widgets into the tab layout.
findings.verification_note: Line range (91-357) and 267-line length confirmed exact via Read. Corrected the group count in the title from 6 to 7 to match what the method (and the finding's own recommendation) actually enumerates.

findings.title: 12 inline QPushButton stylesheets in settings.py duplicate each other and, in 2 cases, near-duplicate src/ui/styles.ButtonStyles
findings.detail: Verified each cited block against src/ui/styles.py. Only 2 of the 12 are close matches to existing ButtonStyles constants, and even those are NOT byte-for-byte identical: btn_save (lines 67-73) matches ButtonStyles.PRIMARY (styles.py:27-34) except it omits PRIMARY's `QPushButton:disabled` rule; btn_cancel (77-83) matches ButtonStyles.SECONDARY (styles.py:37-44) with the same omission. The other ~10 blocks (btn_test 200-208, btn_restore 259-266, btn_export 271-278, btn_import 283-290, btn_refresh_log 466-473, btn_open_log_folder 479-486, btn_clear_log 492-499, btn_check_update 601-609, btn_download 644-652, btn_cancel_download 657-664, install-button 902-909) do NOT match any existing ButtonStyles constant (different hex shades, paddings, font-sizes, disabled handling) ‚ÄĒ but several are byte-for-byte identical to EACH OTHER within this same file: btn_export (271-278) == btn_refresh_log (466-473) [blue #3498db]; btn_import (283-290) == btn_open_log_folder (479-486) [gray #95a5a6]; btn_clear_log (492-499) == btn_cancel_download (657-664) [red #e74c3c]. So the real duplication is 3 undocumented intra-file style families plus 2 near-duplicates of the centralized constants ‚ÄĒ not '12 duplicates of ButtonStyles' as originally framed.
findings.recommendation: Replace btn_save/btn_cancel with ButtonStyles.PRIMARY/SECONDARY directly (the added :disabled rule is a harmless improvement). Add 3 new named constants to src/ui/styles.py's ButtonStyles class to cover the intra-file duplicate families: e.g. INFO_SMALL (blue #3498db, no disabled rule, font-size 11px) for export/refresh-log, SECONDARY_SMALL (gray #95a5a6 bg, white text) for import/open-log-folder (btn_test needs the same base plus its own min-height:26px), and DANGER_SMALL (red #e74c3c, no disabled rule) for clear-log/cancel-download. Add a new INSTALL constant (purple #9b59b6) for the 902-909 block. Then reference the constants from all call sites instead of inline setStyleSheet calls.
findings.verification_note: Read src/ui/dialogs/settings.py and src/ui/styles.py side by side and diffed every cited line range. All 11 line citations in the original finding were accurate down to the exact line, but the claim that all ~12 blocks 'duplicate constants already defined in ButtonStyles' was overstated ‚ÄĒ only 2 are near-duplicates (and not byte-identical), while the rest duplicate each other, not the centralized module. Rewrote detail/recommendation to reflect the actual duplication structure; kept severity high since a dozen near-identical inline stylesheets in one file is still a real, sizeable maintenance problem.

findings.title: dataclass, Optional, QCursor, QThread, pyqtSignal, and UpdatePackageActionText are imported but never used
findings.detail: Confirmed via full-file grep: `from dataclasses import dataclass` (line 5), `from typing import Optional` (line 6), `QThread`/`pyqtSignal` (line 13), `QCursor` (line 14), and `UpdatePackageActionText` (line 30) have zero other occurrences anywhere in the file body ‚ÄĒ they were left behind when CloseConfirmDialog and the update-checker thread/dataclass were extracted into settings_close_confirm_dialog.py and settings_update_helpers.py. There are also 8 stray blank lines at 34-41 (not 6 at 36-41 as originally stated) directly before the class definition at line 42, also left over from that extraction.
findings.recommendation: Remove the 5 unused imports and collapse the stray blank block (lines 34-41) to a single blank line; add flake8/ruff F401 to CI so future extractions don't leave orphaned imports.
findings.verification_note: Grepped the whole file for each symbol name ‚ÄĒ all 5 are import-only, confirming the finding. Corrected the blank-line count/range from 'lines 36-41, 6 lines' to the actual 'lines 34-41, 8 lines' after re-reading that exact span with line numbers.

findings.title: _launch_installer reaches directly into main_window.engine.active_tunnels/tunnel_configs instead of using an accessor
findings.detail: Confirmed exact: to build the 'N active tunnels will disconnect' warning before installing an update, lines 952-958 do `main_window.engine.active_tunnels` and `main_window.engine.tunnel_configs.get(tid, {})` directly ‚ÄĒ SettingsDialog now has intimate knowledge of TunnelEngine's internal dict shapes. Any change to how MainWindow/TunnelEngine track active tunnels silently breaks this unrelated dialog.

findings.title: TunnelConfigDialog.init_ui builds all 7 form sections in one ~210-line method

Name field (85-88), connection-mode radios (90-117), Bastion-host section (119-161, with its own 'Žč§Ž•ł žóįÍ≤į Ž≥Ķžā¨' template-copy button), RDS/target section (163-193), DB-engine/environment combos (178-217), local-port + tunnel-test button (219-234), and DB-auth section + integrated-test button (236-281) are all built inline against one shared form_layout, with no per-section decomposition despite the existing '--- Section ---' header labels marking the boundaries.

findings.title: Environment combo box relies on two hand-maintained, position-coupled index<->string dicts instead of QComboBox item data

The same file shows the correct pattern two combo boxes earlier: combo_db_engine.addItem("MySQL", "mysql") (line 180) + combo_db_engine.currentData()/findData() (lines 182-183, 394).

findings.title: _available_tunnels silently swallows any exception from config_mgr.load_config() and returns []
findings.detail: Confirmed exact: `except Exception: return []` (lines 347-348) with no logging means a corrupt config file, a permissions error, or a future bug in load_config() all look identical to 'no other SSH tunnels exist yet' ‚ÄĒ the 'Žč§Ž•ł žóįÍ≤į Ž≥Ķžā¨' button just quietly disables with no diagnostic trail. Confirmed both schedule_dialog.py (line 26/29: `from src.core.logger import get_logger` / `logger = get_logger(__name__)`) and tunnel_status_dialog.py (line 21/23, same pattern) use get_logger for equivalent situations, so this file is the outlier.

findings.verification_note: Confirmed the except block at the exact cited lines, and confirmed both comparison files (schedule_dialog.py, tunnel_status_dialog.py) do use get_logger while tunnel_config.py has no logger import at all.

findings.title: _test_db_only and _test_integrated repeat the same encryptor-lookup + _TempCredentials + dialog.exec() sequence
findings.detail: Confirmed exact: both methods independently do `if hasattr(self.parent(), 'config_mgr'): encryptor = self.parent().config_mgr.encryptor` (lines 483-484, 513-514), then build a _TempCredentials(...) (486-490, 517-521), then `dialog = self._start_connection_test(...); dialog.exec()` (492-496, 523-527) ‚ÄĒ only the TestType and per-field validation differ. _test_tunnel_only (417-455) shares the trailing dialog-construct+exec pattern too.

findings.title: type_colors dict is computed and stored in _color but the value is never used (suppressed with # noqa: F841)
findings.detail: Confirmed exact: type_colors (218-224) maps 5 event types (connected/disconnected/reconnected/reconnecting/error) to distinct hex colors, and `_color = type_colors.get(event.event_type, "#000000")  # noqa: F841` (line 226) computes the intended color per the comment 'žÉČžÉĀžĚÄ ŪĖ•ŪõĄ ŪĖČ ŽįįÍ≤ĹžÉČžóź žā¨žö© žėąž†ē' (line 225) ‚ÄĒ but _color is never passed to setForeground(). Instead a hardcoded ternary (line 227: darkGreen if connected/reconnected else black) is applied, so disconnected/reconnecting/error events all render identically in black despite the dict implying they should be visually distinct.

findings.title: Max-reconnect-attempts QSpinBox (range 1-20, default 5) is built almost identically here and in settings.py

findings.title: Default group color literal '#3498db' is duplicated inline in the same expression
findings.detail: Confirmed exact: `self._selected_color = group_data.get('color', '#3498db') if group_data else '#3498db'` repeats the same hex literal twice in one line (line 29).

findings.title: ScheduleEditDialog._setup_ui constructs the entire dialog (task-type selector, basic info, backup page, SQL page, schedule tabs, enable checkbox, buttons) in one ~300-line method
findings.detail: Confirmed exact: _setup_ui runs lines 164-464 (301 lines, immediately followed by _connect_signals at 466). Within it: task-type radio group (172-188), basic-info form (190-207), a QStackedWidget with a full backup-settings page (209-246) and a full SQL-editor page including result-format/output/timeout/retention fields (248-324), a schedule-settings QTabWidget with simple (daily/weekly/monthly/hourly with conditional day/dow/minute/time widgets, 332-412) and advanced (cron, 414-441) tabs, plus the final enable checkbox and Save/Cancel buttons (446-464). This is the file's largest method by far.

findings.verification_note: Line range, length, and section breakdown confirmed exact via Read; no changes needed.

findings.title: Dangerous-SQL-pattern detection (DROP/TRUNCATE/DELETE-without-WHERE/UPDATE-without-WHERE) is embedded as regex logic directly in the dialog class
findings.detail: Confirmed: DANGER_PATTERNS (class attribute, lines 137-143, preceded by an explanatory comment at 132-136) and _check_dangerous_query() (lines 484-509) implement non-trivial SQL-safety analysis ‚ÄĒ including a documented edge case about statement-splitting needed to avoid a negative-lookahead regex false negative across multiple statements ‚ÄĒ inside a QDialog. Note the cited 132-509 range spans two non-contiguous chunks (132-143 and 484-509) with ~340 unrelated UI-construction lines in between; the actual defect is confined to those two chunks. This is domain/business logic that has nothing to do with building a dialog UI, and can't be reused or unit-tested independently of PyQt.
findings.recommendation: Move DANGER_PATTERNS and the per-statement danger-checking logic into a small src/core module (e.g. src/core/sql_safety.py) with a pure function `find_dangerous_sql_warnings(sql_text: str) -> list[str]`; have the dialog just call it and update the warning label.
findings.verification_note: Confirmed both sub-ranges (132-143, 484-509) exactly via Read. Flagged that the outer line_start/line_end bracket a large amount of unrelated code in between ‚ÄĒ kept the outer bounds since they're not wrong per se, but added the sub-range breakdown for precision.

findings.title: _browse_result_output_dir and _browse_output_dir are near-identical directory-picker methods
findings.detail: Confirmed: both methods (511-518 and 534-541 respectively, with the unrelated _on_cron_changed method at 520-532 in between) do `current = <line_edit>.text() or os.path.expanduser("~"); dir_path = QFileDialog.getExistingDirectory(self, <title>, current); if dir_path: <line_edit>.setText(dir_path)` ‚ÄĒ differing only in which QLineEdit and dialog title is used.
findings.recommendation: Extract a shared `_browse_dir(self, line_edit: QLineEdit, title: str)` helper and have both call sites pass their specific line edit/title.
findings.verification_note: Confirmed both method bodies exactly via Read. Noted that the cited 511-541 range includes an unrelated method (_on_cron_changed, 520-532) between the two duplicated ones; the actual duplicated code is 511-518 and 534-541.

findings.title: _save() mixes validation branching for two task types with a ~20-argument ScheduleConfig construction in one 110-line method
findings.detail: Confirmed exact: the method runs lines 610-719 (110 lines) and validates name/tunnel, then branches deeply on is_sql_task (626-681) to validate either SQL-specific fields (query, result format/output dir, danger-pattern confirmation) or backup-specific fields (schema, output dir, tables/retention), assigning shared local variables (sql_query, result_format, output_dir, tables, retention_count, ...) with different defaults down each branch, before constructing a ScheduleConfig with exactly 20 keyword arguments (694-717: id, name, tunnel_id, schema, tables, output_dir, cron_expression, enabled, retention_count, retention_days, last_run, next_run, task_type, sql_query, result_format, result_output_dir, result_filename_pattern, query_timeout, result_retention_count, result_retention_days).
findings.recommendation: Split into _validate_and_build_sql_task() and _validate_and_build_backup_task(), each returning a small struct of just the fields relevant to that task type, then assemble the common ScheduleConfig fields once in _save(). Consider grouping SQL-only and backup-only fields into nested sub-dataclasses on ScheduleConfig itself to reduce the flat parameter count.
findings.verification_note: Line range (exactly 110 lines) and the 20-keyword-argument count confirmed exactly via Read; no changes needed.

findings.title: refresh_databases() and execute_sql() independently re-implement the same direct/running-tunnel/temp-tunnel host-port resolution
findings.detail: Confirmed exact: refresh_databases (lines 173-185) and execute_sql (lines 236-250) both branch on `is_direct` -> `self.engine.is_running(tid)` -> else create a temp tunnel, with nearly identical bodies; they differ only in whether progress lines are appended and whether the temp server handle is a local variable (refresh_databases) or self.temp_server (execute_sql). A fix to one copy (e.g. how create_temp_tunnel failure is handled or how the temp server is cleaned up) can easily be applied to only one method and silently miss the other.
findings.recommendation: Extract a shared `_resolve_connection(self) -> tuple[str, int, Optional[Any]]` returning (host, port, temp_server_or_None) with its own progress-message side effects, and have both callers use it.
findings.verification_note: Both cited sub-ranges confirmed exact and near-identical via Read; no changes needed.

findings.title: refresh_databases() runs DB connector business logic synchronously inside the dialog, unlike execute_sql() which delegates to a worker
findings.detail: Confirmed exact: create_rust_db_connector(...) (188-197), connector.connect() (198), connector.get_schemas() (201), and connector.disconnect() (202) are called directly from a QDialog method. The same class's execute_sql() correctly hands equivalent DB work off to SQLExecutionWorker (a QThread, instantiated at line 266), so this dialog is internally inconsistent about where DB access logic lives.
findings.recommendation: Move the schema-listing call behind a small worker/thread (mirroring SQLExecutionWorker) or a src/core helper invoked asynchronously, so all DB access in this dialog goes through one consistent path.
findings.verification_note: Confirmed the direct connector calls and the contrasting SQLExecutionWorker usage at the exact cited lines via Read; no changes needed.

findings.title: Broad except-Exception blocks only surface str(e) in the dialog's transient text box, with no logger call
findings.detail: Confirmed exact: both `except Exception as e: self.output_text.append(f"‚ĚĆ žė§Ž•ė: {str(e)}")` blocks (lines 211-212 in refresh_databases, 281-282 in execute_sql) swallow the full traceback. Confirmed test_dialogs.py has no logger import at all (only PyQt6 widgets and db_core_service imports at the top), unlike schedule_dialog.py and tunnel_status_dialog.py, which both instantiate `logger = get_logger(__name__)` at module scope.
findings.recommendation: Add `from src.core.logger import get_logger` and `logger = get_logger(__name__)` at module scope (consistent with the rest of the dialogs package) and call logger.exception(...) alongside the existing UI message in both except blocks.
findings.verification_note: Confirmed both except blocks at the exact cited lines and confirmed the complete absence of any logger import in test_dialogs.py via Read; no changes needed.

findings.title: TunnelManagerUI is a ~1,220-line god-class owning every app concern
findings.detail: Verified against the current file (1282 lines total). A single QMainWindow subclass implements: window/tray init (init_ui 124-211, init_tray 239-276), tunnel CRUD (add_tunnel_dialog/edit_tunnel_dialog/duplicate_tunnel/delete_tunnel, 579-663), group CRUD (add_group_dialog/_edit_group_dialog/_delete_group, 526-576), connection lifecycle and auto-connect (_ensure_tunnel_running/start_tunnel/stop_tunnel/_auto_connect_tunnels, 715-806, 1131-1184), mysql_config_editor login-path registration (_register_login_path/_remove_login_path, 761-805), DB dialogs/SQL editor/export/import/orphan-check/migration wizards (680-712, 1196-1281), scheduled-backup menu plumbing (896-987, gated by SCHEDULE_FEATURE_ENABLED=False at line 23), tunnel-monitor status callbacks (993-1046), schema-diff dialog (1052-1060), column-width persistence (1062-1118), startup update-checker wiring (1120-1129, 1186-1194), and window repaint scheduling (1091-1103). All sub-ranges checked line-by-line and match the described method boundaries exactly. Note: the scheduled-backup block is currently dead in production (SCHEDULE_FEATURE_ENABLED is hardcoded False), which slightly narrows the 'everything touches this class' argument, but the remaining concerns (CRUD, connection lifecycle, wizards, monitoring, column persistence, tray, update-checker) are all live code and still justify the god-class classification.
findings.recommendation: Split into composed collaborators owned by a slimmer TunnelManagerUI: a TunnelActionsController (CRUD + start/stop/auto-connect + login-path registration), a WizardLauncher (export/import/migration/schema-diff/orphan-check entry points), and a TrayController (tray icon, notifications, schedule submenu). Keep TunnelManagerUI responsible only for layout/widget wiring and delegating signals to these collaborators.
findings.verification_note: Confirmed accurate; all cited line ranges match the file exactly. Added the caveat that the schedule-menu block is currently unreachable because SCHEDULE_FEATURE_ENABLED=False, but this doesn't change the overall verdict.

findings.title: Identical DB-credential-missing guard block repeated 6 times (not 5)
findings.detail: The exact same block ‚ÄĒ `user, _ = self.config_mgr.get_tunnel_credentials(tunnel['id']); if not user: QMessageBox.warning(self, "Í≤ĹÍ≥†", "DB žěźÍ≤© ž¶ĚŽ™ÖžĚī ž†Äžě•ŽźėžĖī žěąžßÄ žēäžäĶŽčąŽč§..."); return` ‚ÄĒ is copy-pasted verbatim in `_on_tree_db_connect` (383-390), `_test_direct_connection` (453-459, uses `user, password = ...` but the same guard shape and identical warning text), `open_sql_editor` (1199-1205), `_context_rust_core_export` (1217-1223), `_context_rust_core_import` (1240-1246), and `_context_orphan_check` (1263-1269). A grep for the warning string confirms exactly 6 occurrences (lines 388, 457, 1203, 1221, 1244, 1267), one more than the finding originally counted.
findings.recommendation: Extract a `_require_db_credentials(self, tunnel) -> tuple[str, str] | None` helper that shows the warning and returns None on failure; call it from all six sites (including `_test_direct_connection`) instead of repeating the check.
findings.verification_note: Original finding undercounted occurrences (said 5, actually 6 ‚ÄĒ missed _test_direct_connection at lines 453-459). Corrected title/detail/recommendation accordingly; severity (medium) still appropriate.

findings.title: Five near-identical 'build RustDumpWizard then call one action' blocks
findings.detail: Confirmed exactly as described: `open_rust_dump_export` (680-687), `open_rust_dump_import` (689-696), `_context_rust_core_export` (1229-1235), `_context_rust_core_import` (1252-1258), and `_context_orphan_check` (1275-1281) all construct `RustDumpWizard(parent=self, tunnel_engine=self.engine, config_manager=self.config_mgr[, preselected_tunnel=tunnel])` and then invoke exactly one of `start_export()`/`start_import()`/`start_orphan_check()`.
findings.recommendation: Add one private helper, e.g. `_launch_rust_dump_wizard(self, action: str, tunnel: dict | None = None)`, that builds the wizard once and dispatches via `getattr(wizard, action)()`, and call it from all five entry points.
findings.verification_note: Confirmed accurate; minor line-range adjustments (each block is 1 line shorter than cited since the finding included the trailing blank line before the next method) but not worth flagging as an error.

findings.title: Column count hardcoded as literal 7 instead of derived
findings.detail: Confirmed: `_load_column_ratios` validates saved ratios with `if ratios and len(ratios) == 7:`, duplicating the column count implicitly defined by `self._default_column_ratios` (line 197, exactly 7 entries: `[0.05, 0.20, 0.08, 0.25, 0.12, 0.10, 0.20]`). Nothing ties these together.
findings.recommendation: Replace the literal `7` with `len(self._default_column_ratios)`.

findings.title: refresh_table mixes an extracted helper with inlined widget construction at the same level
findings.detail: Confirmed: the power button is built via the extracted `_build_power_button(tunnel, is_active)` helper (326-335, called at line 358), but the edit/delete button container in the same loop is built inline with raw `QWidget`/`QHBoxLayout`/`QPushButton` calls (361-376) instead of a matching helper.
findings.recommendation: Extract a `_build_manage_buttons(tunnel)` helper mirroring `_build_power_button`, returning the container widget, and call it symmetrically alongside `_build_power_button`.

findings.title: Success/failure tray-notification branching duplicated across two methods
findings.detail: Confirmed: `_run_schedule_now` (944-958) and `_show_backup_complete_notification` (974-987) both branch on a `success` flag to call `self.tray_icon.showMessage(title, message, icon, duration)` with the same Information/Warning icon choice and near-identical title strings. Note: this whole scheduled-backup subsystem is currently dormant since `SCHEDULE_FEATURE_ENABLED = False` (line 23) means `self.scheduler` is never constructed and these methods are unreachable in production today ‚ÄĒ the duplication is real but its current runtime impact is nil until the feature flag is flipped back on.
findings.recommendation: Extract a shared `_notify_backup_result(self, title_ok, title_fail, name, success, message)` used by both call sites.
findings.verification_note: Code matches exactly as described. Added the caveat that this code path is currently feature-flagged off, which doesn't invalidate the finding but is relevant context for prioritization.

findings.title: Two competing styling systems coexist; the actively-used one is not theme-aware
findings.detail: Confirmed via repo-wide grep: `TableStyles`, `TabStyles`, `DialogStyles`, `ProgressStyles`, `TextEditStyles`, `GroupBoxStyles`, `InputStyles`, `Colors`, `apply_button_style`, `apply_label_style`, `get_dynamic_button_style`, and `get_dynamic_label_style` have zero references anywhere in the repo outside of styles.py itself. Meanwhile the hardcoded, non-theme-aware `ButtonStyles`/`LabelStyles` (23-153) ARE actively used via `setStyleSheet(ButtonStyles.PRIMARY)` etc. in `main_window.py`, `dialogs/tunnel_config.py`, and `dialogs/group_dialog.py` (confirmed by grep for `ButtonStyles\.` / `LabelStyles\.`). Because a widget's own `setStyleSheet()` wins over the app-wide stylesheet from `get_full_app_style()` (confirmed: `get_full_app_style`, lines 708-753, wires in dynamic input/table/tab/list/scrollbar/progress/groupbox styles but never button or label styles), every button/label styled this way is permanently pinned to the light-theme hex values and never updates on dark-mode switch.
findings.recommendation: Migrate the three call-site files off `ButtonStyles`/`LabelStyles` onto the already-written `get_dynamic_button_style(variant)`/`get_dynamic_label_style(variant)`, then delete the now-fully-dead `TableStyles`/`TabStyles`/`DialogStyles`/`ProgressStyles`/`TextEditStyles`/`Colors`/`apply_button_style`/`apply_label_style`, and finally `ButtonStyles`/`LabelStyles`/`InputStyles`/`GroupBoxStyles` once migration is complete.
findings.verification_note: Core claim fully confirmed. One correction: the finding lists `widgets/tunnel_tree.py` as one of the four active call sites of ButtonStyles, but that file only has a dead/unused `from src.ui.styles import ButtonStyles` import (line 14) with zero actual `ButtonStyles.X` usages ‚ÄĒ only 3 files (main_window.py, tunnel_config.py, group_dialog.py) actually call it. This doesn't weaken the finding (if anything it adds one more piece of dead code to clean up).

findings.title: _show_context_menu builds two unrelated menus in one method
findings.detail: Confirmed: a single 69-line method builds the entire group context menu (connect-all/disconnect-all/edit/delete, 310-326) and the entire tunnel context menu (duplicate/edit/test/db-connect/sql-editor/export/import/orphan-check/delete, 328-363) behind one `if/elif` on `item_type`.
findings.recommendation: Split into `_build_group_context_menu(menu, group_id)` and `_build_tunnel_context_menu(menu, tunnel_data)`, leaving `_show_context_menu` as a thin dispatcher that resolves `item_type` and delegates.

findings.title: Widget bypasses its own signal pattern to reach config_mgr via Qt parent-chain walk
findings.detail: Confirmed: `_save_collapsed_state` walks the live Qt ancestor chain (`parent = self.parent(); while parent: ... parent = parent.parent()`) looking for an object with a `config_mgr` attribute and calls `parent.config_mgr.save_group_collapsed_state(...)` directly, instead of using the signal-based decoupling this class uses everywhere else (confirmed: `tunnel_moved_to_group`, `group_edit_requested`, `group_delete_requested`, etc. are all declared as pyqtSignal at lines 22-37 and used via `.emit()`). This silently no-ops if the widget tree shape ever changes.
findings.recommendation: Add a `group_collapsed_changed(group_id: str, collapsed: bool)` signal, emit it from `_on_item_expanded`/`_on_item_collapsed` instead of calling `_save_collapsed_state` directly, and have `TunnelManagerUI` (which already owns `config_mgr`) persist it in `_connect_tree_signals`.

findings.title: run() inlines three structurally different task branches
findings.detail: Confirmed: the single `run()` method (58-172) handles `export_schema` (66-92), `export_tables` (94-122), and `import` (124-161) inline, each branch re-declaring its own set of nested progress/detail/status/raw-output callback closures before invoking `RustDumpExporter`/`RustDumpImporter`.
findings.recommendation: Split into `_run_export_schema()`, `_run_export_tables()`, and `_run_import()` private methods; keep `run()` as a short dispatcher on `self.task_type` plus the shared cancel/exception handling in the try/except/finally.

findings.title: Identical progress-callback closures redefined in all three run() branches
findings.detail: Confirmed: the one-line pass-through closures `detail_callback`, `table_status_callback`, and `raw_output_callback` are redefined verbatim inside the `export_schema` branch (71-78), the `export_tables` branch (99-106), and the `import` branch (129-136), which additionally repeats the same shape for `metadata_callback`/`table_chunk_progress_callback` (138-142).
findings.recommendation: Hoist these as bound methods on the class (e.g. `self._on_detail`, `self._on_table_status`, `self._on_raw_output`, `self._on_metadata`, `self._on_table_chunk_progress`), defined once, and pass them by reference from every branch instead of re-declaring closures.

findings.title: MigrationAnalyzerWorker constructor takes 16 parameters, 14 of them boolean flags mirrored three times
findings.detail: Corrected count from the original finding: `__init__` (16-36) accepts `connector`, `schema`, plus 14 individual `check_*` boolean flags (check_orphans, check_charset, check_keywords, check_routines, check_sql_mode, check_auth_plugins, check_zerofill, check_float_precision, check_fk_name_length, check_invalid_dates, check_year2, check_deprecated_engines, check_enum_empty, check_timestamp_range) ‚ÄĒ 16 parameters total, not 17/15 as originally stated. Each is assigned to `self.x = x` one line at a time (39-55), then forwarded again by name, one per line, to `analyzer.analyze_schema(...)` in `run()` (62-79). The same 14 identifiers are typed out three separate times (constructor parameter, `self.` attribute, forwarded keyword argument), so adding a 15th check requires editing all three places plus every call site that constructs this worker.
findings.recommendation: Bundle the 14 `check_*` flags into a single `MigrationCheckOptions` dataclass (or plain dict) and accept one `options: MigrationCheckOptions` parameter instead; `run()` can then forward it as `analyzer.analyze_schema(self.schema, **asdict(self.options))` or pass the object straight through if `analyze_schema` is updated to accept it.
findings.verification_note: Original finding miscounted the parameters: actual count is 16 total parameters (connector + schema + 14 check_* flags), not 17 with 15 booleans. Corrected numbers in title/detail/recommendation; severity (high) remains justified given the triple-duplication maintenance burden.

findings.title: Three of four imported symbols are never used
findings.detail: Confirmed via grep: `AnalysisResult`, `CleanupAction`, and `ActionType` are imported (5-7 as `from src.core.migration_analyzer import (MigrationAnalyzer, AnalysisResult, CleanupAction, ActionType)`) but never referenced as live symbols anywhere in the file. `AnalysisResult`/`CleanupAction` appear only inside `#` type comments at lines 13 and 99, and `ActionType` isn't referenced at all ‚ÄĒ `action.action_type.value` (line 134) is an attribute access on an instance, not the class itself. Only `MigrationAnalyzer` is actually instantiated.
findings.recommendation: Remove the three unused imports, or if the intent was self-documentation, turn the comments into real type annotations (e.g. `actions: List[CleanupAction]`, `analysis_complete = pyqtSignal(object)  # type: AnalysisResult` via a proper stub).

findings.title: CleanupWorker's dry_run parameter is accepted, validated, then ignored
findings.detail: Confirmed: `CleanupWorker.__init__` accepts `dry_run: bool = True` (100) solely to raise `RuntimeError` when it is `False` (103-107); the value is never stored (no `self.dry_run`), and `run()` unconditionally calls `analyzer.execute_cleanup(action, dry_run=True)` (128) no matter what was passed in.
findings.recommendation: Since real execution is permanently disabled per the Rust Core migration, drop the `dry_run` parameter entirely (constructor takes no such argument) and document/rename the class as preview-only, rather than keeping a parameter whose only effect is asserting its own value.

findings.title: FixWizardWorker repeats the same vestigial dry_run pattern as CleanupWorker
findings.detail: Confirmed identical shape to `CleanupWorker` in `migration_worker.py`: `FixWizardWorker.__init__` (74-92) accepts `dry_run: bool = True`, never stores it as `self.dry_run`, and only uses it to `raise RuntimeError` when `False` (83-87). `run()` hardcodes `dry_run=True` at both places that matter ‚ÄĒ `changer.execute_safe_charset_change(..., dry_run=True, ...)` (119-124) and `executor.execute_batch(self.steps, dry_run=True)` (159).
findings.recommendation: Apply the same fix as `CleanupWorker` (drop the vestigial `dry_run` parameter) in both files together, since they now share the exact same guard-then-ignore shape and should be cleaned up consistently.

findings.title: run() mixes process-lifecycle plumbing with protocol-event dispatch
findings.detail: Confirmed: the 77-line `run()` spawns the helper process, writes/closes stdin, starts the stderr-drain thread, then reads stdout lines while running a 6-branch `if/elif` dispatch on `event.event` (`result`/`error`/`phase`/`table_progress`/`row_progress`/`issue`, 129-152) with inline checkpoint bookkeeping, before finally waiting on the return code and handling cancellation/exceptions.
findings.recommendation: Extract the event-type branching into a `_dispatch_event(self, event)` method so `run()` reads as 'spawn ‚Üí stream lines ‚Üí dispatch each ‚Üí finalize', separating process lifecycle from JSONL protocol interpretation.

findings.title: Identical cancel()/_cancelled scaffolding repeated across three worker classes
findings.detail: Confirmed: `ValidationWorker.cancel()` (48-50), `MetadataLoadWorker.cancel()` (118-120), and `AutoCompleteWorker.cancel()` (165-167) are byte-for-byte identical (`self._cancelled = True`), and all three classes separately declare `self._cancelled = False` in `__init__` plus repeat `if self._cancelled: return`/`if not self._cancelled: emit(...)` guards throughout `run()`.
findings.recommendation: Introduce a small `CancellableWorker(QThread)` base class owning `_cancelled` and `cancel()`, and have all three (and similarly-shaped workers such as `UpdateDownloadWorker`) inherit from it instead of redeclaring the same two lines each.

findings.title: Unused typing import
findings.detail: Confirmed via grep: `from typing import List, Optional` (line 7) is imported but neither name is used as a real type annotation anywhere in the file ‚ÄĒ `List`/`Optional` only appear inside `#` comments (`# List[ValidationIssue]`, `# List[Dict]`), and every constructor parameter uses plain types or bare `= None` defaults (e.g. `schema: str = None`).
findings.recommendation: Remove the unused import, or annotate the optional parameters as `Optional[str]` if the intent was documentation.

findings.title: _test_db and _test_integrated duplicate ~80% of their connection-resolution and cleanup logic
findings.detail: Confirmed: both `_test_db` (52-141) and `_test_integrated` (143-230) implement the identical three-way connection resolution ‚ÄĒ direct mode uses `remote_host`/`remote_port`; an already-running tunnel uses `engine.get_connection_info`; otherwise a bastion-reachability check (`test_target_reachable_from_bastion`) followed by `create_temp_tunnel`/`get_temp_tunnel_port` ‚ÄĒ and the identical `finally` cleanup (disconnect connector, `close_temp_tunnel`, emit `test_finished`). The two methods differ mainly in progress-message wording and result-string assembly.
findings.recommendation: Extract a shared `_resolve_connection(self) -> tuple[str, int, temp_server | None]` (or a small context manager wrapping temp-tunnel creation/cleanup) used by both `_test_db` and `_test_integrated`, leaving each method with only its own result-formatting logic.

findings.title: SQLExecutionWorker (arbitrary SQL-file execution) lives in a module named for connection testing
findings.detail: Confirmed: `SQLExecutionWorker` (262-356) opens a `.sql` file, splits it into statements, executes each via a cursor, and formats result rows ‚ÄĒ a general SQL script runner, not a connection test. It sits in `test_worker.py` alongside `ConnectionTestWorker`/`TestType`, whose sole purpose is connectivity testing.
findings.recommendation: Move `SQLExecutionWorker` into its own module (e.g. `sql_execution_worker.py`), grouped with the SQL-editor-related workers rather than the connection-test workers.

findings.title: Five identical lazy-import trampoline functions whose names shadow the classes they return
findings.detail: Confirmed: `QApplication()`, `QIcon()`, `ConfigManager()`, `TunnelEngine()`, and `TunnelManagerUI()` (99-126) are five functions with the identical shape (`from <module> import <Name> as <alias>; return <alias>`). Their names also collide with the classes they return: call sites like `app_cls = QApplication()` (line 251 in `main()`, line 195 in `run_ui_smoke_check()`) read as if a `QApplication` instance is being constructed, when it actually returns the *class*, requiring a second call (`app_cls(sys.argv)` at line 257, or `app_cls([...])` at line 206) to instantiate.
findings.recommendation: Replace the five functions with one helper, e.g. `def _lazy_class(module_path: str, name: str)`, called as `_lazy_class("PyQt6.QtWidgets", "QApplication")`; if kept as separate named functions, rename them to avoid shadowing the class names (e.g. `get_qapplication_class`).
